title: Dichroanthus cinereus
intro: Dichroanthus cinereus, commonly known as the yellow horned poppy or yellow horned cuckoo flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, exhibiting health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in soothing digestive issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Dichroanthus cinereus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its pharmacological properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Dichroanthus cinereus is often prepared as a tea or dried extract, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and infusions.
taxonomy: Dichroanthus cinereus is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, including many species of trees, shrubs, and herbs. The genus Dichroanthus is relatively small and comprises several species, with Dichroanthus cinereus being one of the most well-known. Dichroanthus cinereus is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found growing in dry, rocky areas. Its classification reflects its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.
